behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: Start at Unit. This behavior is grouping logic over parsed records keyed by trace_id; the failure can be reproduced inside one function/object with no required module handoff. Rejected Integration: behavior text only requires grouping correctness, not cross-module state propagation. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: no real OS/network/subprocess, no model output, no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a single grouping function/object a mixed in-memory list of log records from multiple service names with overlapping trace IDs; assert one group per trace ID and membership independent of service field.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: Start at Unit. Core failure boundary is timestamp ordering/skew-compensation algorithm; it can fail without any module wiring. Rejected Integration: behavior text targets ordering correctness itself, not a handoff bug between components. Rejected System: no requirement for real clocks/OS events; deterministic in-memory timestamps are sufficient. No LLM or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Call the timeline ordering function with synthetic records from multiple services where timestamps differ within and beyond 500ms skew windows; assert final order follows the skew rule.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: this behavior includes both detection and report flagging, so a real failure can occur at detector->reporter handoff (e.g., spike computed but not surfaced in report schema). Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process modules suffice. (b) no AI/ML output dependency. (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejected System due behavior text focusing on metric threshold/report wiring, not infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run anomaly detection service plus report builder together with in-memory latency samples where p99 is just above/below 3x baseline; assert anomaly flag presence/absence in the produced report payload.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: failure appears at parser->pipeline->metrics/report handoff (bad record handling plus skipped-count propagation), not just inside a pure parse function. Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess is required; malformed strings can be injected in-process. (b) no AI/ML dependency. (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real filesystem/network ingestion.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the in-process ingestion pipeline with a mixed batch of valid and malformed JSON lines; assert process continues, malformed records are excluded from output, and skipped counter matches malformed input count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: Integration->System proof: this is a real throughput/backpressure claim tied to runtime scheduling, queueing, and wall-clock behavior under load; in-process mocks can hide drops/lag that occur only under real execution conditions. Rejected Agentic: behavior text is infrastructure/performance, not model-output quality. Rejected Workflow: no multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual tool process in a load test harness that emits real input at 10,000 entries/sec for a sustained window; measure ingest vs processed counts and end-to-end lag, asserting zero drops and bounded backlog growth.
